evidence: Two problems with the finding. (1) Wrong location: resample.ts:90-110 is the STATELESS resamplePcm16 function (lines 91-109), not StreamingPcm16Resampler (lines 111-160). The stateless function DOES taper at both edges (firDecimate comment, lines 47-53: 'their amplitude tapers due to the missing context, which is the accepted trade-off for stateless chunk processing'), but that function is only used in TESTS (telnyx.test.ts:78, twilio.test.ts:89, audio.test.ts). (2) The class kimi names — StreamingPcm16Resampler, which IS the production path (browser index.ts:481,544; twilio.ts:201,307; telnyx.ts:602,672; smartpbx.ts:275,458; server index.ts) — ALREADY carries history forward, which is exactly kimi's suggested fix. process() at resample.ts:139-157 prepends history: 'combined.set(this.history,0); combined.set(input, historyLength)' then runs firDecimate over the combined buffer and retains 'keepHistory = Math.min(FIR_TAPS-1, combined.length)' = up to 126 samples (full filter half-window) as next history (lines 156-157). The test at audio.test.ts:230-251 EXISTS to prove this: feeding constant 10000-valued 320-sample chunks, statefulSwing < 700 while statelessSwing > 3000 — i.e. the streaming class is essentially flat (no left-edge taper), the stateless one rings. So the central claim 'history carry-forward needed' is already satisfied for the left/leading edge. A residual TRAILING-edge effect does remain (each chunk's last ~63 output samples are emitted using truncated right taps and are never re-corrected, since next chunk's firstNewOutput skips the history region, lines 146-153), but the test bounds its worst-case swing at <700 of 10000 (~7%) on a pathological constant-step signal, only on the downsample/FIR path (ratio>1); upsample uses linearInterpolate (no FIR). The '<127-sample chunks, audible' qualifier is unsubstantiated — production chunks are 160 (20ms@8k) / 320 (20ms@16k) samples.
real_fix: No code change required; kimi's prescribed fix (history carry-forward) is already implemented in StreamingPcm16Resampler.process (resample.ts:139-157) and verified by audio.test.ts:230-251. The only residual is a small, test-bounded (<7% swing) trailing-edge taper on the downsample path because chunk-tail outputs are emitted before future context arrives. If ever judged audible, the clean fix is to delay emission: hold back the last halfTaps/ratio output samples (~halfTaps source samples worth) until the next chunk supplies their right context, instead of emitting fullOutput.subarray(firstNewOutput) to the end. Not warranted at current evidence (no audible-artifact repro, no sub-127-sample production chunk). Note the finding's cited line range (90-110) points at the wrong function and should be corrected to 111-160 if re-filed.
files: packages/voice/src/audio/resample.ts:111-160 (StreamingPcm16Resampler.process, history carry-forward at 139-157; residual trailing-edge emission at 146-154); contrast stateless resample.ts:91-109 + firDecimate taper comment 47-53; proof test packages/voice/src/audio/audio.test.ts:230-251
